title: Octarrhena papuana
taxonomy: Octarrhena papuana is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Octarrhena, which is a group of orchids. The species is further classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land rather than in water or air. The classification of Octarrhena papuana is based on its physical characteristics, such as its leaf and flower structure. This classification helps scientists to understand the relationships between different plant species and their evolutionary history.
uses: Octarrhena papuana is used in traditional medicine in Papua New Guinea, where it is valued for its potential medicinal properties.
